mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques for efficient memory management and request handling. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm repository to your local Helm setup, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.16.0

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository.
> Updates the Helm repositories to fetch the latest charts.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to define its custom resources in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.16.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.16.0

```

> Installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
Creating an example CR allows you to deploy a sample model with vLLM for testing.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt2'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample vLLM resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt2'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the deployed vLLM instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM instances and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and its associated resources. Note that this may result in data loss if any models or configurations are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> Uninstalls the vLLM release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.16.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Drains the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Uncordons the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the namespace may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure that vLLM is installed in the correct namespace using 'helm list --namespace vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create VLLM resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD for vLLM may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.16.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The model specified in the CR may not be available or there may be insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' and ensure the specified model is supported.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for vLLM.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the vLLM CR or add more nodes to the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM instance listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm'.

  replicas: 1
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
